reasoning: The stock market return over shorter horizons approximates a random walk with high noise. The YFinance statistical tool provided a combined estimate of P(>74.02) = 0.57 due to strong seasonal priors. However, the recent price performance for Bunge (BG) has been quite volatile, dropping rapidly from roughly $75 on April 3 to $67 on April 8 before rebounding to $74.02 on April 11. Bunge's pending acquisition of Viterra and upcoming Q1 earnings/dividend dates also inject idiosyncratic volatility. Given the YFinance model as my primary anchor, I am slightly more confident the price will be higher in the very short term (factoring in the potential continuation of the latest 3-day bounce). In compliance with the rule that uncertainty should increase over time, my estimates start at 0.54 for the closest resolution date (April 20) and steadily regress toward 0.51 for the furthest resolution date (October 10).
